title: History, Geography & World Civilizations
subject: History & Social Studies
description: Explore ancient civilizations, North American settlement history, trade routes, cartography, and world geography.
learningObjectives: Understand major milestones in North American history and Indigenous heritage.
Read world maps, cardinal directions, latitude, and longitude.
Trace how historical trade routes shaped modern society.

lessons.title: Latitude, Longitude & Maps
lessons.content: Latitude lines run horizontally parallel to the Equator (measuring North and South). Longitude lines run vertically from Pole to Pole (measuring East and West). Together, they form a coordinate grid that pinpoints any location on Earth!

lessons.title: Early Trade Routes & Homestead Pioneers
lessons.content: Pioneers and early settlers relied on rivers, natural mountain passes, and trade trails to travel west. Understanding history helps us learn how earlier generations survived and built resilient communities.

flashcards.definition: Imaginary horizontal lines measuring distance North or South of the Equator.
Imaginary vertical lines measuring distance East or West of the Prime Meridian.
The imaginary circle around the middle of the Earth at 0 degrees latitude.

questions.question: Which imaginary line sits at 0 degrees latitude and divides the Earth into Northern and Southern Hemispheres?
What coordinate grid system is formed by combining lines of latitude and longitude on a globe?

practicalChallenge.title: Map Maker & Latitude Expedition
practicalChallenge.instruction: Draw a map of your neighborhood or property. Include a Compass Rose (N, S, E, W) and a map key legend!
